title: Refraction by Thin Lenses — PYP Mastery

questions.question: The diagram shows a ray of light passing through a thin converging lens. Which labelled distance is the focal length $f$?n[Diagram showing rays parallel to axis meeting at F]
options: The distance from the optical centre to the principal focus
The distance from the principal focus to the focal plane
The distance across the widest part of the lens
The distance from the object to the lens
questions.explanation:
Standard IGCSE diagrams identify $f$ as the distance from the centre of the lens to the point where parallel rays meet.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: A student measures the size of the bright spot formed by a lens at different distances $d$ from a piece of paper.nn d / \text{ cm}$|$5$|$10$|$15$|$20$n--- | --- | --- | --- | ---nSpot diameter /$\text{mm}$|$8$|$4$|$1$|$5$nnWhat is the approximate focal length of the lens$?
options: $15\text{ cm}$
$10\text{ cm}$
$5\text{ cm}$
$20\text{ cm}$
questions.explanation:
The focal point is the point where the spot is smallest and brightest. According to the data, this occurs at a distance of $15\text{ cm}$.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: Which diagram correctly shows parallel rays of light passing through a diverging lens?
options: Rays bending away from each other as if starting from a point behind the lens
Rays bending towards each other to meet at a point
Rays passing through without any change in direction
Rays reflecting off the front surface of the lens
questions.explanation:
A diverging lens causes parallel incident rays to spread out, and their paths can be traced back to a virtual focal point on the same side as the incident light.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: In a converging lens, what is the 'focal plane'?
options: A plane perpendicular to the principal axis passing through the focal point
The flat surface of the lens
The plane containing the principal axis
The surface on which the lens is placed
questions.explanation:
The focal plane is defined as the plane that passes through the focal point $F$ and is perpendicular to the principal axis.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: Light of a single frequency is used with a thin converging lens. Which term describes this light?
options: Monochromatic
Dispersed
Divergent
Polychromatic
questions.explanation:
Visible light of a single frequency (one colour) is described as monochromatic. Polychromatic light like white light contains many frequencies.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.
